jobPostingInfo.title: Jr. CAM Development Engineer (On-Site Mahwah, NJ)
jobPostingInfo.jobDescription:
Job Description:

The Junior CAM Development Engineer begins their career journey by supporting the global CAM development team in building and maintaining CAD/CAM automation solutions. They perform structured assignments, apply established procedures, and gain hands‑on experience with software and automated manufacturing equipment. Working under guidance, they contribute to projects in the medical/dental technology domain while developing technical skills and collaborating with colleagues to ensure reliable production outcomes.

PRIMARY DUTIES:

  • Develop new CAM-automation processes; maintain and update existing processes, keeping them current and as efficient as possible.
  • Recommend milling strategies and collaborate with team to find best solutions in CAM-automation.
  • Participate in conference calls, as needed, with global teams on solutions.
  • Provide regular written and verbal status progress reports and project updates; communicate next steps to management as required.
  • Actively participate in the development and roll-out of new system worldwide.
  • When needed, support production operations, quickly respond to production issues, conduct root cause analysis, define temporary work-arounds, develop and implement long-term solutions.
  • Support prototyping and voice of customer experimentation.
  • Document work and development through different document type according to company standards.
  • Other duties as assigned.

Job Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in engineering, manufacturing, or related field.
  • Minimum 1 year of experience in similar engineering role; Exposure to CNC machining, CNC CAM programming preferrable OR combined equivalent experience in the following areas: engineering experience, hands-on CNC machining experience, CNC CAM programming experience using PowerMill or equivalent software.
  • Must be able to work on site in Mahwah, NJ, 5 days per week.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS:

  • Proficiency in PowerMILL, PowerSHAPE (Delcam/AutoDesk).
  • Working knowledge of milling and turning industries, eg. develop cutting data, develop/find milling tool.
  • Expertise in CAM automation and industrial solutions.
  • Familiarity with manufacturing automation processes.
  • Experience with 5-axis CNC controllers (e.g., Fanuc, Roeders, Heidenhain).
  • Programming knowledge in C#, JavaScript.
  • Experience with Post Processors.

CRITICAL SUCCESS FACTORS AND KEY CHALLENGES:

  • Understanding of documentation for Medical Device regulation (preferred).
  • Ability to identify, discern, and analyze issues related to CAM and recommend/implement solutions in a timely manner.
  • Ability to perform under pressure and meet deadlines set by the supervisor.
  • Team player and collaborative with both co-workers and the supervisor.
  • Fluent in English, excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.

KEY COMPETENCIES:

  • Pragmatic problem solver with attention for detail and completeness
  • Action Oriented and Drives Results
  • Collaborates and Communicates Effectively
  • Nimble Learning
  • Brings fresh ideas and curiosity to solving manufacturing challenges while learning from experienced engineers.
  • Builds foundational skills in CAD/CAM automation by applying established procedures and tools.
  • Learns to contribute to innovation through structured assignments and guided problem‑solving.
  • Collaborates effectively with supervisors and project teams, showing openness to feedback and teamwork.
  • Develops reliability in delivering tasks that support consistent production outcomes.

#LI-BS1

IND123

Target Market Salary Range:

Actual compensation packages take into account a wide range of factors that are unique to each candidate, including but not limited to geographic location; skill sets; relevant education and certifications; depth of experience; performance; and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed reasonable estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled. At Envista, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. The total compensation package for this position may also include an annual performance bonus, medical/dental/vision benefits, 401K match, and/or other applicable compensation plans.

$81,000 - $99,000

Operating Company:

Nobel Biocare

An Agency must obtain advance written approval from Envista's internal Talent Acquisition or Human Resources team to submit resumes, and then only in conjunction with a valid fully-executed contract approved by the Global Talent Acquisition leader and in response to a specific job opening. Envista will not pay a fee to any Agency that does not have such agreement and written approval in place.
